data.body:
INVESTIGATION (read-only; no code changed). Three questions about the plan-review gate, answered by reading the criteria/workflow/engine sources.

Q1 — Does plan review check that plans cite EVIDENCE for new patterns drawn from OSS research?
Finding: Only partially, and in the inverse direction. No criterion requires citing evidence/research when a plan justifies a new pattern by appeal to OSS/industry practice. Closest is T1 (src/rebar/llm/reviewers/plan_review_T1.md:10, routing criteria_routing.json:637-671): an AGENTIC overlay that checks (a) prior_art was CONSIDERED before going custom, (b) novelty is justified vs an established approach (anti-repackaging/Rule-of-Three), (c) capability assertions ('library supports X') resolved. T1 pushes you to research prior art before building custom; it does NOT validate an OSS-sourced pattern claim. Surviving T1 findings route to coach Move #2 'prior-art research' (passes.py:527-530). Adjacent: G6 (positive rationale for chosen approach), A1 (anti-cargo-cult). None mandate OSS citations.

Q2 — Does plan review evaluate whether the described implementation matches an ANTIPATTERN?
Finding: Yes, substantially, across several criteria — and it screens BOTH the described implementation approach AND ticket-document quality.
- A1 (plan_review_A1.md:10; routing criteria_routing.json:888-926): the dedicated named-antipattern screen (DSO decider set): golden-hammer, cargo-cult, resume-driven, premature-optimization, NIH, premature-abstraction/Rule-of-Three, YAGNI, config-surface-proliferation. Codebase-grounded (grep evidence required). FP guards: Justified-Complexity needs affirmative evidence; FP7 carve-out = don't flag NIH when rebuilding a designated POC/experiment/reference. Scope: applies_at.levels=['task'], suppress_types=['bug'].
- G6 (plan_review_G6.md): mechanism antipatterns — incomplete logic/data-flow, unhandled edge/empty/concurrent/failure cases, check-then-act-that-is-really-TOCTOU, solving wrong problem, ignoring better alternatives.
- E5 (plan_review_E5.md:10): testing antipatterns — self-authored-oracle/change-detector (always MAJOR), proxy-validation (mock bypasses the new/defaulted path).
- T1: reinvention/anti-repackaging. T8: instruction-locality/pink-elephant (undefined enums/skills/agents). T11: migration antipatterns (blocking DDL, non-batched backfill, non-resumable, dual-write gaps). T12: rollout antipatterns (100%-flip, no rollback).
Note A1's named set is a fixed list (not an open-ended smell catalog); A1 runs on tasks only, not bugs/containers.
